item.content:encoded:
The ending feels a bit too dark for Wondertainment at least in my interpretation. Not that I'm opposed to a darker take on Wondertainment (in fact the Misters are probably my favorite thing they've made) but this seems extra dark. Even when their doing messed up stuff it's usually more attributed to thoughtlessness / shitty safety standards than outright malice. Though I do note that we technically don't know who the guy at the end is working for. Maybe he's with Marshall, Carter and Dark's product testing group? Sounds more like something they'd do.

But the writing is good and I love it when SCPs start out cute/funny then plunge into pure nightmare fuel so +1 even though I might be using a different headcanon then the author intended.

Never mind, I just read the explanation for this skip on the author's totally awesome author page and I was just jumping to conclusions. Whoops.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While D-Class abuse is an honored, timeworn tradition that I don't mind seeing in other articles, I don't particularly care much for writing it in myself. I'll probably slaughter a few here or there if it feels necessary for the story, but if it isn't explicitly called for, I intend to keep them relatively safe.

Making one of them puke their (or someone else's) guts out is totally fair game tho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I firmly expected the ending to be something gruesome happening yet again to the D's.

I was pleasantly surprised. This is good and tense. Some additional follow-through could give the Foundation an opportunity to look good while still fully keeping in-character, but leaving off on a suspenseful note, just before anything like that can happen, is healthier for the imagination.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should make a separate experiment log page for this, and add more PoI's. At one point I think you should have 100 D-Class trying this experiment, and have something weird come from it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the best new SCP articles I've seen in a while. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh hey, I ran across that one when I was looking to see what other lollipop-based skips there were already! Makes me kinda wonder, if you activated both at the same time, could you make yourself believe everything you made the other subject say?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I swear I've seen another skip just like this the only difference being that the D-class was able to escape after using one of the lolipops

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thankya kindly! I'm glad that you - and from the way the rating has gone the past couple of days, at least a modicum of other folks - are likin' this. It being my first mainlister, I gotta admit that I was pretty worried.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is quite a creative scip, and the ending with the child genuinely helped me understand what this was supposed to do. It had the perfect twist of interesting and creepy, and it genuinely painted Dr. Wondertainment as the bad guy(s?).

That easily earned my upvote.
